items.text: crates/liminal/src/aion/mod.rs contains only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports.
AionSurfaceError defined in crates/liminal/src/aion/error.rs with variants for dispatch, signal, and streaming failures.
All public types in the aion module implement Debug; AionSurfaceError implements std::error::Error via thiserror.
Channel naming constructors in channels.rs enforce the aion.{kind}.{namespace}.{id} convention and reject malformed inputs.
ActivityRequest and ActivityResult types defined in types.rs with activity type, input payload, task queue, and timeout fields.
dispatch.rs opens a conversation on the task queue channel, selects a worker via the routing function, and links to the selected worker process.
Worker crash during activity execution is detected via process link exit signal and triggers re-dispatch to another available worker.
Dispatch conversation lifecycle maps to Aion activity states: opened to ActivityScheduled, worker selected to ActivityStarted, response to ActivityCompleted, link failure to ActivityFailed.
Activity message types defined in codec.rs with typed channel validation per ADR-002.
No global consumer group rebalancing exists in the dispatch path; adding or removing workers is handled by the routing function adapting to subscription changes.
Per-workflow signal channel created on workflow start via channels.rs naming convention aion.signal.{namespace}.{workflow_id}.
Signal payloads validated against the workflow's declared signal types at publish time, not consume time.
Workflow process receives signals as standard BEAM messages through its signal channel subscription.
Signal channel torn down when workflow completes or is terminated.
Per-workflow history channel created with naming convention aion.history.{namespace}.{workflow_id}.
Recorder publishes each workflow event to the history channel after writing to the event store.
History subscribers can read from an arbitrary cursor position for replay from the durable channel.
History consumers are decoupled from the event store API; they read exclusively through channel subscription.
Workers register by subscribing to task queue channels with capacity metadata (max concurrent activities, activity type filter).
Worker health is determined by subscription liveness; no heartbeat protocol or health-check endpoint exists.
Worker capacity declarations are available to the routing function for load-aware dispatch.
Worker deregistration is automatic on subscription close or process crash.
All channel operations in the integration surface are recorded by the Aion recorder and replayable by the resolver.
Zero-hop dispatch: embedded Aion and liminal in the same beamr instance dispatch activities through in-process channels with no serialization boundary.
No file in crates/liminal/src/aion/ exceeds 500 lines.
